brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is a numerical stabilization scheme (nonequilibrium-entropy limiters/filters that erase spurious oscillations and control artificial dissipation) for the lattice Boltzmann fluid solver, whereas Paper B's machinery is the physics of elastically-driven phase separation/coarsening in alloys, where lattice-misfit elastic interactions govern precipitate morphology and kinetics. The only overlap is the word 'lattice' (lattice Boltzmann vs. crystal lattice spacing) and the generic presence of an evolving scalar field; no role-for-role correspondence between a limiter/dissipation-control scheme and an elastic-energy-driven coarsening process holds.
